proposed_hypotheses.text: pfs_months is a non-negative continuous outcome with a right-skewed distribution and contains no censoring indicator; treating it as a continuous outcome via OLS/Spearman correlations is appropriate.

analyses.code: df['pfs_months'].describe(); (df['pfs_months']==0).sum(); df['pfs_months'].nlargest(20)
analyses.result_summary: n=50000; mean=3.43, median=3.32, sd=2.23, range 0.00-15.33. 3653 patients (7.3%) have pfs=0; only 6 patients have pfs>=14, ruling out a censoring cap. Treated as continuous outcome.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Several binary features are associated with pfs_months; a univariate screen will identify candidate prognostic and/or treatment markers.

analyses.code: for each binary feature: stats.ttest_ind(pfs[x==1], pfs[x==0])
analyses.result_summary: Strongest binary main effects on pfs_months: feature_023 (mean diff -1.44 mo, p~0), feature_011 (-0.92, p~0), feature_016 (+0.78, p=4.8e-151), feature_028 (-0.81, p=2.9e-69), feature_018 (+0.32, p=1.6e-53), feature_031 (-0.20, p=2.4e-24), feature_021 (-0.19, p=9.1e-18), feature_022 (+0.19, p=1.9e-10). feature_002, feature_020, feature_027 not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among continuous features, feature_015 (range 30-90, mean 65) shows the strongest association with pfs_months and likely represents a baseline prognostic continuous variable; other plausible continuous prognostics are feature_025 and feature_017.

analyses.code: stats.spearmanr for each continuous feature vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: feature_015 vs pfs_months: Spearman r=+0.769 (p~0) — by far the dominant continuous association, near-linear (slope +0.17 mo per unit). feature_017: r=-0.125 (p=1.8e-172). feature_025: r=+0.100 (p=6.3e-111). All other continuous features have |r|<0.02 and p>0.001.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 (adenocarcinoma vs squamous) and feature_001 (smoking: never/former/current) are categorical features associated with pfs_months: adenocarcinoma will have longer pfs than squamous, and current smokers will have shorter pfs than former or never.

analyses.code: ANOVA across feature_001 levels; t-test for feature_006; ANOVA for feature_014.
analyses.result_summary: feature_006: adenocarcinoma mean pfs=3.67 vs squamous 2.81 (F=1522, p~0). feature_001: never 3.67, former 3.61, current 2.99 (F=433, p=3.7e-187). feature_014 has 3 levels (0,1,2) with monotonic decrease in pfs (4.33 -> 3.19 -> 2.15; F=3141, p~0) consistent with an ordered prognostic variable (e.g., performance status / stage).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the strongest prognostic features, feature_016, feature_018, and feature_025 will show positive independent associations with pfs_months while feature_023, feature_011, feature_014, hist_squamous, smoking_current, feature_017, and feature_031 will show negative associations.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_015 + feature_023 + feature_011 + feature_014 + hist_squamous + smoking_current + smoking_never + feature_017 + feature_016 + feature_025 + feature_018 + feature_031 (and selected others)
analyses.result_summary: R^2=0.894. Independent positive: feature_015 +0.169/unit (p~0), feature_016 +0.892 (p~0), feature_025 +0.463 (p~0), feature_018 +0.322 (p~0). Independent negative: feature_023 -1.42, feature_014 -1.10, feature_011 -0.91, hist_squamous -0.72, smoking_current -0.55, feature_031 -0.20, feature_017 -0.073. feature_022, feature_028, feature_021 lose significance after adjustment, suggesting they are confounded by stronger features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_016 and feature_018 are the strongest candidates to represent treatments because they retain large positive effects on pfs_months after multivariable adjustment, whereas feature_022/feature_028/feature_021 likely represent prognostic markers or noise.

analyses.code: Examine adjusted coefficients and prevalence of binary features.
analyses.result_summary: feature_016 (~13% prevalence) adjusted +0.89 mo; feature_018 (~35% prevalence) adjusted +0.32 mo. Their positive adjusted effects, plausible prevalence ranges for systemic anticancer therapies, and consistency with NSCLC clinical patterns make them the leading treatment candidates. Other 'positive' features (feature_022, feature_021) lose significance after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The main effect of feature_016 on pfs_months is heterogeneous across binary baseline features; in particular, feature_031, feature_028, and feature_005 attenuate or abolish the benefit of feature_016.

analyses.code: OLS adding feature_016 x modifier interaction terms one by one (adjusting for full base model).
analyses.result_summary: Strongest negative interaction terms for feature_016: x feature_031 coef=-1.62 (p~0), x feature_028 coef=-0.91 (p=3.5e-78), x feature_005 coef=-0.91 (p=1.2e-56). These three binary features each abolish or reverse the feature_016 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_016 is concentrated in patients with feature_031=0 (mean pfs in feature_016=1 minus feature_016=0 is large and positive), and is null or negative in patients with feature_031=1.

analyses.code: Stratified two-sample t-tests of pfs by feature_016 within feature_031 strata.
analyses.result_summary: feature_031=0: mean(feature_016=1)=4.87 vs mean(=0)=3.33, diff=+1.54, p=4.8e-301. feature_031=1: mean(=1)=3.19 vs mean(=0)=3.34, diff=-0.15, p=3.1e-04. Direction reverses across feature_031 strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_031=0 patients, the benefit of feature_016 is further restricted to those with feature_028=0 and feature_005=0; presence of either feature_028 or feature_005 abolishes the benefit.

analyses.code: Three-way stratification: feature_016 effect within {feature_031=0, feature_028=0, feature_005=0} and complementary cells.
analyses.result_summary: feature_031=0, feature_028=0, feature_005=0: feature_016 effect = +1.65 mo (mean 5.01 vs 3.36, n0=22214, n1=3266, p~0). All other 5 cells: effect ranges from -0.53 to +0.23 mo, none clinically meaningful and only one even nominally significant (feature_031=1, feature_028=1, feature_005=0: -0.53, p=0.013, n1=116).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_018 on pfs_months is heterogeneous: it is attenuated or abolished by feature_031, feature_028, feature_005, and (univariately) by smoking=never; smokers and squamous patients may benefit more.

analyses.code: OLS interaction screen feature_018 x modifier.
analyses.result_summary: Strongest interactions for feature_018: x feature_031 coef=-0.58 (p~0), x smoking_never coef=-0.33 (p=3.2e-65), x smoking_current coef=+0.25 (p=5.1e-65), x feature_028 coef=-0.34 (p=1.2e-27), x feature_005 coef=-0.33 (p=1.6e-16), x feature_022 coef=-0.21 (p=1.3e-24), x hist_squamous coef=+0.11 (p=1.7e-12).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stratified estimates will show feature_018 benefit nearly absent in feature_031=1 and in feature_028=1 and feature_005=1 strata, and present (mean diff ~+0.5 mo) only in patients without these markers; smokers benefit numerically more than never smokers.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of pfs by feature_018 within each modifier stratum.
analyses.result_summary: feature_031=0: feature_018 effect +0.57 (p=5e-85); feature_031=1: +0.01 (p=0.77). feature_028=0: +0.34 (p=1e-58); feature_028=1: -0.14 (p=0.13). feature_005=0: +0.33 (p=5e-55); feature_005=1: 0.00 (p=0.98). smoking=never +0.01 (ns), former +0.30 (p=1e-26), current +0.51 (p=4e-41). Effect concentrated in same {f31=0, f28=0, f05=0} strata as feature_016.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_016 and feature_018 act synergistically: the pfs_months benefit when both are present (=1) is much larger than the sum of their individual effects, and individual main effects largely vanish once a feature_016*feature_018 interaction term is included.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ base + feature_016 + feature_018 + feature_016*feature_018
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_016 main coef = -0.0005 (ns), feature_018 main coef = -0.0014 (ns), interaction (feature_016*feature_018) coef = +2.534, p~0. Unadjusted joint means: f16=0/f18=0 mean=3.33; f16=0/f18=1 mean=3.33; f16=1/f18=0 mean=3.21; f16=1/f18=1 mean=5.77 (n=2246). Both features must be present for any benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The combination effect (feature_016=1 AND feature_018=1) on pfs_months is large and positive only in feature_031=0; in feature_031=1 the combination provides no benefit.

analyses.code: Stratified joint means of pfs_months across feature_016 x feature_018 within each feature_031 stratum.
analyses.result_summary: feature_031=0: combo (f16=1 & f18=1) mean=7.88 (n=1237) vs reference (f16=0 & f18=0) mean=3.33 — diff +4.54 mo. feature_031=1: combo mean=3.18 (n=1009) vs reference mean=3.33 — diff -0.16 mo (no benefit).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_031=0, the combination benefit is further confined to feature_028=0 and feature_005=0; presence of either of these features abolishes the combination benefit.

analyses.code: Three-way joint stratification of (combo vs not) by feature_031 x feature_028 x feature_005.
analyses.result_summary: Combo (feature_016=1 & feature_018=1) effect: feature_031=0/feature_028=0/feature_005=0: +4.89 mo (n_combo=1154, p~0); feature_031=0/feature_028=0/feature_005=1: +0.25 mo (p=0.49); feature_031=0/feature_028=1/feature_005=0: -0.36 mo (p=0.30); feature_031=1/feature_028=0/feature_005=0: -0.13 mo (p=0.06); feature_031=1/feature_028=1/feature_005=0: -0.80 mo (p=0.026). Benefit isolated to the triple-zero cell.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Define 'eligible' = feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0. Adjusted for prognostic factors, the combination feature_016=1 AND feature_018=1 confers approximately +4.9 months pfs_months only within eligible patients; in ineligible patients the combination has no effect.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ base prognostics + feature_016 + feature_018 + combo*eligible + main effects of f31, f28, f05
analyses.result_summary: Final model R^2=0.9785. combo*eligible coef = +4.922 (95% CI 4.900-4.944, p~0). Once this term is in, feature_016 (coef +0.002, p=0.75) and feature_018 (coef +0.001, p=0.80) main effects vanish, confirming the combination benefit is fully captured by combo within eligible.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the eligible subgroup, the combination effect is homogeneous across other binary covariates (e.g., feature_023, feature_011, hist_squamous, smoking, feature_022) — i.e., no further binary modifier substantially expands or contracts it.

analyses.code: Within eligible patients, stratified t-tests of combo effect by every other binary covariate.
analyses.result_summary: Combo effect within eligible across other binary modifiers: feature_023=0 +5.06 vs =1 +4.80; feature_011=0 +4.94 vs =1 +4.70; feature_022=0 +4.92 vs =1 +4.76; hist_squamous=0 +4.99 vs =1 +4.90; smoking_current=0 +5.08 vs =1 +4.93; smoking_never=0 +4.96 vs =1 +4.09 (small group n_combo=21). All sub-stratum estimates fall in 4.7-5.1 range; no major secondary modifier identified.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within eligible patients, the magnitude of the combination effect is mildly amplified by feature_015 (each unit of feature_015 adds slightly to combo benefit), but the combination benefit is positive across the entire feature_015 range.

analyses.code: Within eligible, OLS with combo*feature_015 interaction and base prognostics.
analyses.result_summary: Within eligible: combo main coef=+4.09 (p~0); combo*feature_015 coef=+0.013 per unit (p=2e-40). Magnitude ranges from ~+4.5 mo at feature_015=30 to ~+4.9 mo at feature_015=90 — a small refinement, the qualitative subgroup definition does not change.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 acts as a strong prognostic factor (negative effect on pfs_months ~-1.4 mo) but does not modify the combination treatment effect; its interaction with combo or eligibility is small relative to its main effect.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with feature_023 main and feature_023 x modifier interactions; also stratified t-tests of feature_023 effect within feature_016=1/feature_018=1 strata.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted main effect of feature_023 = -1.42 mo (p~0) is by far its dominant signal. Within eligible-combo group, feature_023 effect remains ~-1.7 mo. Mild interactions appear with feature_015, feature_014, smoking_current but |coef|<0.13. feature_023 acts as a prognostic factor independent of treatment heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 is a prognostic factor (~-0.9 mo) without a positive treatment-effect subgroup; no stratum exists in which feature_011=1 patients have better pfs than feature_011=0.

analyses.code: Within every binary covariate stratum, two-sample t-test of feature_011 effect on pfs_months.
analyses.result_summary: feature_011 effect was negative in every stratum tested (range approx -0.6 to -1.2 mo); no stratum produced a positive effect, supporting feature_011 as a pure prognostic feature rather than a treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 is at most a minor prognostic / mild positive factor for pfs_months and is not part of the combination treatment effect; its effect is small (<+0.3 mo) and roughly homogeneous across the eligibility-defining strata.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of feature_022 effect on pfs_months by feature_031 x feature_028 x feature_005.
analyses.result_summary: feature_022 effect: range 0.13-0.59 across strata; most precise stratum (f31=0, f28=0, f05=0): +0.18 (p=5e-5). Loses significance in adjusted multivariable model. Magnitude small relative to combo effect (+4.9 mo).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_021 is a small negative prognostic factor (~-0.2 mo) and does not act as a treatment; its effect is roughly uniform across feature_031 and feature_028 strata.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of feature_021 effect by feature_031 x feature_028.
analyses.result_summary: feature_021 effect: -0.19 in feature_031=0/feature_028=0; -0.18 in feature_031=1/feature_028=0; -0.33 in feature_031=0/feature_028=1; -0.48 in feature_031=1/feature_028=1. Uniformly slightly negative (ns vs prognostic in adjusted model). Not a treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive screen of all binary x binary interactions on pfs_months (adjusting for prognostic factors) will identify only feature_016 x feature_018, feature_016 x feature_031, feature_016 x feature_028, feature_016 x feature_005, feature_018 x feature_031, feature_018 x feature_028, and feature_018 x feature_005 as substantively large; no second independent treatment combination exists in the data.

analyses.code: All-pairs binary x binary OLS interactions adjusting for base prognostic features.
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions by |coef| (excluding duplicates): feature_016 x feature_018 +2.53, feature_016 x feature_031 -1.62, feature_016 x feature_028 -0.92, feature_016 x feature_005 -0.91, feature_018 x feature_031 -0.58, feature_018 x feature_028 -0.35, feature_018 x feature_005 -0.33, feature_028 x feature_031 +0.21, feature_018 x feature_022 -0.21. All other pairs have |coef|<0.15. No additional treatment-like combination signal beyond feature_016 x feature_018.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90) is the dominant prognostic continuous variable for pfs_months: its slope (~+0.17 mo per unit) is highly robust and accounts for ~57% of the variance in pfs_months by itself.

analyses.code: Univariate OLS pfs_months ~ feature_015 and quantile means.
analyses.result_summary: Univariate: slope +0.170, r=+0.757, R^2~0.573. Quantile means rise monotonically from 0.74 (q1) to 6.55 (q10) of pfs_months. Slope unchanged in multivariable model (+0.168, p~0). Single largest contributor to outcome variance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_025 (range 1.7-5.5, mean 3.8) is a continuous laboratory-like prognostic variable with a positive association with pfs_months that is independent of feature_015 and is not a treatment effect modifier.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS effect of feature_025 and feature_025*combo interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted main effect of feature_025 +0.463 mo per unit (p~0). Interaction with combo within eligible -0.058 (p=0.003) — very small relative to combo main effect. feature_025 acts essentially as a baseline prognostic continuous covariate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final treatment-effect heterogeneity hypothesis: in NSCLC patients with feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0, receipt of the combination (feature_016=1 AND feature_018=1) increases pfs_months by approximately +4.9 months relative to any other combination of feature_016/feature_018 status; in patients with any of feature_031=1, feature_028=1, or feature_005=1, the combination provides no pfs_months benefit (point estimates near zero or slightly negative).

analyses.code: Final model: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_015 + feature_023 + feature_011 + feature_014 + hist_squamous + smoking_current + smoking_never + feature_017 + feature_025 + feature_031 + feature_028 + feature_005 + feature_016 + feature_018 + combo_eligible (where combo_eligible = feature_016*feature_018*eligible)
analyses.result_summary: combo_eligible coef = +4.922 mo (p~0); feature_016 main +0.002 (p=0.75); feature_018 main +0.001 (p=0.80). Confirmed the combination benefit is exclusively realized within the eligible subgroup. R^2 = 0.9785. Direction (+), magnitude (~+4.9 mo), and statistical support (p<<0.001) all consistent with a true treatment-by-biomarker interaction.
